name: Get Past a Gatekeeper to the Decision-Maker
description: When the agent calls a target organisation on behalf of the operator and reaches a receptionist, executive assistant, or main-line gatekeeper, navigate to the right decision-maker with honesty and respect — not tricks.
disclaimer: Skills assume the call is solicited by the operator. NEVER use these to power a mass-cold-call campaign without the operator's explicit per-prospect authorisation.

context.when_to_use: Operator has asked the agent to reach a specific person (named or by role) at a company, and the call is intercepted by a gatekeeper. The operator has a legitimate, named reason for the call (introduction, follow-up, referral, prior touch).
preconditions: Operator has authorised this specific outreach (not a mass campaign).
Agent has the target's name or, at minimum, a precise role title.
Agent has a one-sentence honest reason for the call ready.
Agent has the referring party's name on hand if this is a referral.

principles: The gatekeeper is a colleague, not an obstacle. Treating them as an obstacle is the fastest way to be permanently blocked.
Be specific about who you want and why. Vagueness reads as sales; specificity reads as legitimate business.
Never lie about who you are, who you represent, or what the call is about. Gatekeepers verify and have long memories.
Match the formality of the receiving organisation. A law firm gatekeeper expects more formality than a startup's front desk.
If the gatekeeper says 'they're not available', accept it on the first ask. Pressing turns a soft no into a hard no.

phrases.opener_named: Hi, this is [agent name] calling from [operator name]'s office. Could you put me through to [target full name], please?
phrases.opener_referral: Hi, this is [agent name] on behalf of [operator name]. [Referrer name] suggested I reach out to [target name] about [topic] — is [target] the right person, and could I be put through?
phrases.opener_role: Hi, this is [agent name] calling for [operator name]. Could you tell me who handles [specific function] there, and whether they're available to speak briefly?
phrases.honest_reason: It's about [one-sentence honest topic]. I'm not selling anything on this call — [operator] just wants to introduce themselves / follow up on [prior touch].
phrases.voicemail_redirect: Thank you. Could you send me to their voicemail, or is there a better time to try them?
phrases.polite_decline_accept: No problem at all — thank you for checking. Could I leave a brief message, or would email be better for a first touch?
phrases.name_request: I want to make sure I send the right thing in writing — could I get your name? Thanks, [name], appreciate it.

tactics.name: Lead with the target's name if you have it
tactics.when: You know exactly who you want to speak to.
tactics.script: Use the `opener_named` phrase. Saying the target's first and last name signals you actually know them, not that you're dialing a list.

tactics.name: Cite the referrer immediately if there is one
tactics.when: This call exists because someone introduced the operator.
tactics.script: Use `opener_referral`. The referrer's name does most of the work — drop it in the first sentence, not deep in the call.

tactics.name: If you only have a role, ask who owns it
tactics.when: Operator only knows 'the head of partnerships' or similar.
tactics.script: Use `opener_role`. Phrase it as a discovery question, not a demand. Gatekeepers respond well to 'help me figure out who I should be talking to'.

tactics.name: Offer the honest reason BEFORE being asked
tactics.when: Gatekeeper pauses or asks 'what is this regarding?'.
tactics.script: Use the `honest_reason` phrase pre-emptively on calls into formal organisations. It disarms the screening question.

tactics.name: Ask the gatekeeper's name and remember it
tactics.when: After the gatekeeper has spent more than ~20 seconds helping.
tactics.script: Use `name_request`. Logging the gatekeeper's name and using it on future calls compounds enormously — within 2-3 calls they recognise you as 'one of the good ones'.

tactics.name: Accept the first soft no
tactics.when: Gatekeeper says target is in a meeting / unavailable / travelling.
tactics.script: Use `polite_decline_accept`. Ask for best time or for voicemail. Do NOT ask twice in the same call to override the answer.

boundaries: Do NOT pretend to be a personal friend, family member, or colleague of the target.
Do NOT claim the target 'is expecting your call' unless it is literally true.
Do NOT use the 'I'll just leave my number, they have my email' trick to extract direct lines.
Do NOT push past a gatekeeper who has clearly said no. Try a different channel (email, LinkedIn) next instead.
Do NOT call back the same day after being told the target is unavailable — that escalates to harassment fast.
success_signals: Gatekeeper transfers the call to the target.
Gatekeeper provides a direct dial, email, or 'best time to call'.
Gatekeeper offers to take a detailed message and confirms it will be passed on.
Gatekeeper confirms the right role/owner for the topic.
failure_signals: Gatekeeper firmly says 'we don't take cold calls — send an email'.
Gatekeeper repeats target is unavailable on a second probe.
Gatekeeper asks the agent to remove them from a list (treat as a hard stop).
Gatekeeper recognises this is the third call this week and is now cold.

exit_strategy.on_success: Note the gatekeeper's name and any callback window they suggested. If transferred, lean into the voicemail or live-call skill depending on what answers.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Thank the gatekeeper warmly by name, ask for the best written channel (general inbox vs. role-specific email), and end the call. Log a 'do not retry by phone for N days' note for the operator.
follow_ups: Email the operator: who answered, gatekeeper's name, what was learned (target's correct role / spelling / best channel).
If a callback window was given, schedule the next attempt inside that window — not earlier.

required_user_info: Operator's name and the name of the company/identity being represented
Target's name OR precise role at the target organisation
One honest sentence about why the call is happening
Whether voicemail is acceptable as a fallback
